title: Google Business Profile Assistant (Long-Term Role)
description:
SEO Ranking Pro (SRP) is seeking a reliable assistant to help manage Google Business Profiles for local service businesses. This role focuses on structured, repeatable tasks that improve how businesses appear in Google Search and Google Maps. This is a long-term position supporting ongoing client accounts, not short-term freelance project work.

Core Responsibilities

Stage 1 — Cleanup & Stabilization

• Review business profile information for accuracy

• Correct business details (hours, categories, services)

• Identify duplicate or conflicting listings

• Assist with verification or reinstatement steps

• Standardize profile information

Stage 2 — Profile Updating & Optimization

• Update services and business descriptions

• Upload and organize photos

• Create and publish Google Posts (templates provided)

• Maintain profile completeness

• Monitor Q&A sections

Stage 3 — Ongoing Maintenance & Visibility Protection

• Perform scheduled profile checks

• Publish recurring posts

• Monitor reviews and alerts

• Update seasonal information

• Report issues or changes

Work follows documented checklists and procedures.

________________________________________

Ideal Candidate

This role is best suited for someone who:

• Works carefully and follows instructions well

• Pays attention to small details

• Is comfortable performing repeatable tasks

• Wants stable, ongoing work

• Communicates clearly and reliably

Prior SEO experience is not required, but consistency and accuracy are essential.

________________________________________

Required Skills & Qualifications

• Strong written English

• Comfortable using Google Docs and spreadsheets

• Ability to follow step-by-step procedures

• Reliable internet connection

• Ability to manage multiple accounts using checklists

• Willingness to learn structured workflows

Preferred (not required):

• Experience with Google Business Profiles

• Local SEO or directory listing experience

• Content posting or virtual assistant experience

________________________________________

Work Structure

• Long-term role supporting ongoing client accounts

• Task-based workflow using standardized procedures

• Training and documentation provided

• Stable workload expected as client base grows

title: Remote Operations & Growth Coordinator (Full-Time, Long-Term)
description:
We are a U.S.-based supplement manufacturing and fulfillment company looking for a high-level Remote Operations & Growth Coordinator to join our team long-term.



This is not a basic VA role.



We are looking for someone who understands operations, fulfillment, CRM systems, and client communication, and who wants to grow into a leadership role over time.



What You’ll Be Responsible For:



• Coordinating manufacturing and fulfillment workflows

• Managing CRM pipelines (Zoho and Monday or similar)

• Tracking inventory and shipment timelines

• Supporting business development follow-ups

• Communicating with U.S.-based clients professionally 
 & Zoom)

• Ensuring no lead, order, or shipment falls through the cracks

• Helping build structured SOPs and processes



You Are a Strong Fit If You:



• Have 3+ years experience in operations, logistics, fulfillment, or CRM-heavy roles

• Have worked with Shopify, Zoho, Salesforce, or similar platforms

• Can speak and write English clearly and professionally

• Are comfortable on phone and video calls

• Think in systems and processes (not just tasks)

• Can stay calm under pressure

• Want long-term stability and growth



This Is NOT For You If:



• You are only looking for short-term freelance work

• You only have appointment-setting experience

• You need constant supervision

Compensation & Growth:

• Competitive rate based on experience

• Clear path to senior operations role

title: Operations & Research Assistant
description:
Please review this job post completely before applying.



Job Title: Operations & Research Assistant

Type: Full-Time

Hours: 40 hrs/week (must overlap EST)

Location: Remote

Start Date: Immediate (within 1 week)



About the Role



We are a U.S.-based real estate investment company, but this role is focused on operations, research, and task support. You do not need real estate experience to succeed here.



We’re looking for a proactive, detail-oriented professional who can manage
, conduct web research, process assigned tasks, and support leadership with projects and decision-making. This is a role for someone resourceful and motivated — not a simple “calendar/email VA.” You’ll be given responsibility and trusted to deliver results with minimal supervision.



What You’ll Do



? Manage
 communication daily through Outlook (respond clearly and keep inbox organized)

? Conduct research on service providers (pricing, reviews, quality, terms) and prepare clear comparison presentations

? Process a variety of assigned tasks — from organizing data and documents to preparing reports and summaries

? Use task management tools (ClickUp, Asana, or similar) to stay organized and track deadlines

? Communicate updates and progress via Slack and other tools

? Join Zoom calls as needed to collaborate or share results

? Quickly learn new tools or processes by researching online or watching tutorials (YouTube, guides, etc.)

? Provide reliable support to leadership by ensuring tasks are completed accurately and on time



Must-Have Skills & Requirements



? 3+ years in an operations, executive assistant, or research-heavy support role

? Excellent English (written and spoken) — professional and clear communicator

? Strong web research skills — able to find, evaluate, and summarize information independently

? Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint)

? Familiarity with task management tools (ClickUp, Asana, Trello, or similar)

? Comfortable using Slack, Zoom, and ChatGPT for communication and task execution

? Highly detail-oriented, organized, and dependable without direct supervision

? Strong workstation setup: desk, chair, reliable desktop/laptop, 1–2 monitors, headset, and camera

? Positive energy, motivated mindset, and the ability to “vibe” well with the team



Nice to Have



? Experience preparing structured presentations (Google Slides, PowerPoint)

? Vendor research or procurement support background

? Prior remote work experience with structured task management systems



Why Work With Us



? Long-term, stable position with consistent hours

? Opportunity to take on diverse tasks and grow into higher-level responsibilities

? Professional team that values independence, results, and reliability

? Supportive work culture with room for personal contribution



How to Apply (Read Carefully)

title: High-Performance Senior SDR (SaaS/Real Estate) – High Commissions + Growth
description:
Are you a "closer" trapped in an "opener’s" body?



We are a fast-growing SaaS company providing mission-critical integration software to the Real Estate industry. We aren’t looking for a robot to read a script; we are looking for a sales hunter who understands the psychology of B2B prospecting and knows how to navigate past gatekeepers to reach decision-makers in the US and EU.



This is a Senior SDR role. If you have mastered the art of the cold call and thrive on turning a "No" into a discovery meeting, we want to talk to you.



What You’ll Do

Hunt & Qualify: Execute high-volume outbound cold calls,
s, and LinkedIn touches to B2B decision-makers.

Own the Calendar: Book high-quality meetings for our Sales Account Executives.

Navigate Tech: Maintain a clean pipeline in our CRM (HubSpot/Salesforce) and leverage sales intelligence tools.

Iterate: Constantly refine your scripts and objection handling based on real-world feedback.



Requirements (Non-Negotiable)

Near-Native English: Your pronunciation must be clear, and your comprehension sharp. You will be speaking with US/EU-based Founders and Executives.

2+ Years B2B Experience: Proven track record in an SDR, BDR, or Cold Calling role.

The "SaaS Mindset": You understand what an integration is and can explain value, not just features.

Home Office: High-speed internet (with backup) and a noise-canceling professional setup.



Big Plus If You Have:

Experience in PropTech or selling to Property Managers.

Familiarity with APIs and software integrations.

A history of hitting or exceeding quotas (be ready to share your wins).



Why Join Us?

Competitive Base + Aggressive Incentives: We pay for quality. High performers earn significantly above market rates.

Long-Term Growth: We are scaling fast. Today’s SDR is tomorrow’s Sales Manager or AE.

Ownership Culture: We don’t micromanage. We provide the tools, and you provide the results.

 • Maintain brand consistency and voice



Required Qualifications

 • Proven results with local SEO (show ranking proof)

 • Experience generating measurable leads for service-based businesses

 • Strong understanding of search intent and buyer psychology

Highly Preferred

 automation knowledge



What Success Looks Like

title: Inventory and Reconciliation Bookkeeper
description:
Join Our Remote Accounting Team

We are a fast-growing, boutique outsourced accounting firm supporting high-growth e-commerce and service-based businesses. Our team works behind the scenes to help dynamic DTC brands stay financially organized, inventory-accurate, and positioned for smart growth.



Many of our clients operate in product-based environments, including brands that assemble or manufacture their own products. While deep manufacturing expertise is not required, familiarity with assemblies and product costing is a strong plus.

We are a fully remote company with a collaborative, detail-oriented culture. We value accountability, clear communication, and continuous improvement.



We are looking for an Inventory & Reconciliation Bookkeeper with e-commerce experience who thrives in structured environments and takes pride in clean, accurate financial records.



About the Role

This role focuses on inventory tracking, reconciliations, and supporting month-end financial processes for e-commerce clients. You will work closely with senior accounting tea
mbers to ensure inventory records, cost tracking, and related reconciliations are accurate and complete.



Some clients maintain assembled or manufactured products, so exposure to Bills of Materials (BOMs), product assemblies, or kit structures is an added advantage.



This is an execution-focused role ideal for a strong bookkeeper with hands-on inventory exposure in an e-commerce environment.



Core Responsibilities

1. Inventory & Transaction Recording

- Record inventory-related transactions, including purchases, sales, returns, write-offs, and transfers

- Maintain accurate inventory records based on established procedures

- Ensure transactions align with vendor invoices, fulfillment reports, and sales platform data

- Flag discrepancies or unusual activity for review



2. Reconciliations

- Perform regular reconciliations between accounting records, inventory reports, and sales channels

- Identify and report variances in inventory quantities or costs

- Prepare supporting schedules for month-end review

- Assist in resolving discrepancies by gathering documentation and coordinating with relevant parties



3. Cost Tracking & Support

- Assist with tracking product costs, including freight and related expenses

- Maintain organized documentation for inventory purchases and adjustments

- Support preparation of Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) schedules

- Prepare inventory-related journal entries for review



4. Manufacturing / Assembly Support

- Maintain and validate Bills of Materials (BOMs) in coordination with senior tea
mbers

- Support costing for assembled or manufactured products

- Reconcile component usage to finished goods output

- Assist in reviewing inventory flows for assemblies, kits, or bundled products



5. Month-End Support

- Maintain inventory roll-forward schedules

- Provide documentation for financial review and reporting

- Ensure inventory balances are supported and reconciled each month



Qualifications

- 3–5 years of bookkeeping experience

- Experience working with e-commerce businesses (Shopify, Amazon, or similar platforms)

- Experience with inventory tracking and reconciliations

- Experience with assemblies, kits, or manufactured inventory preferred

- Understanding of BOM structures and component costing preferred

- Strong understanding of basic accounting principles

- Proficient in Excel (LOOKUPs, Pivot Tables preferred)

- Highly detail-oriented and organized

- Comfortable working independently in a remote environment

- Strong written and verbal English communication skills



What We Offer

- Competitive Pay: $13/hour

- Full-time remote role

- Exciting benefits as an independent contractor

- Performance-based growth opportunities

- A collaborative, supportive team environment

title: Urgent Hiring. Appointment Setter. Needed Immediately
description:
ABOUT US



ZZ Med Spa is a fast growing medical spa company based in Manhattan, New York. We operate multiple Day Spa and Med Spa locations and currently employ over 80 tea
mbers across our organization.



We are expanding our Appointment Setting team and are looking for experienced, confident professionals who are serious about long term work and performance.



ROLE OVERVIEW



As an Appointment Setter, you will be responsible for contacting leads, booking appointments, and maintaining accurate CRM records. This role requires urgency, strong communication skills, and the ability to confidently guide prospects toward scheduling.



This is not an entry level role. We are looking for individuals with real outbound experience who understand follow up discipline and conversion.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



• Conduct high volume outbound calls and SMS outreach to potential customers

• Contact new leads immediately and follow up consistently

• Schedule qualified prospects into our calendars

• Maintain accurate and detailed CRM records

• Handle objections professionally and clearly explain service benefits

• Track daily metrics including call volume, conversion rates, and appointments set



REQUIREMENTS



• Minimum 3 years of experience in appointment setting or outbound sales

• Excellent spoken and written English with clear pronunciation and confident delivery

• Strong persuasion and objection handling skills

• Experience using CRM systems and Microsoft Office

• Organized, disciplined, and goal oriented

• Reliable high speed internet and a professional work environment

• Available full time during New York business hours



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR



We are seeking candidates who are confident on the phone, proactive in follow up, and comfortable working in a performance driven environment. You should be resilient, coachable, and motivated by results.



Qualified candidates will be contacted for the next step in our screening process.



We are planning to onboard quickly.





APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS



To be considered, please include the following:

1. The total number of years you have in appointment setting or outbound sales

2. A short voice recording introducing yourself and summarizing your relevant experience

3. Your updated resume

4. Write “NYC” in the subject line of your application so we know you read the full job description.



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed.

title: Claims Researcher - Copywriter, Researcher, Legal
description:
Are you looking for a mission driven career that helps those people in need?

Are you a hard-working, motivated individual who wants to work with other hard-working, motivated individuals?

Do you want the flexibility to work from home?

Does working for a start-up company with many growth opportunities sound exciting?

Do you work well independently and want a job where you can complete your tasks without being micromanaged?

Most importantly, do you like helping people?



About Us:

We are a veteran advocate company that helps veterans increase their disability ratings through a VA accredited attorney. If you don’t know what this is, it’s the compensation they get for disabilities sustained while they were serving in the US military. We are a startup of almost 100 people and are growing quickly. Come and join this fast paced environment that will make you feel good when you come home from work everyday because you will be helping veterans in need.



Position Overview:

We are seeking a resourceful, intelligent, and adaptable copywriter to join our team. In this role, you will analyze veterans’ cases, conduct in-depth research, and craft compelling, well-structured written materials to support VA disability appeals. The heart of this position is strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and exceptional writing ability, translating complex case details into clear, persuasive narratives that advocate effectively on behalf of veterans.  Help us help the veterans tell their story.



While legal credentials are not required, candidates must demonstrate the ability to think critically, organize information logically, and produce high-quality written work. Graduates from social studies-related disciplines such as International Studies and similar fields often bring strong research, analytical, and writing skills that align well with this role. Additionally, individuals with experience in the Philippine defense industry or backgrounds working closely with military personnel and veterans may be especially well suited, given their familiarity with military culture and demonstrated writing strengths.



If you are detail-oriented, intellectually curious, and motivated to make a meaningful difference through the power of writing, this role could be an excellent fit.



Key Responsibilities:

- Conduct thorough research on veterans’ disability claims, including medical records, legal precedents, and VA policies.

- Analyze case details and synthesize complex information into clear, well-structured memos for VA appeals.

- Work closely with attorneys and tea
mbers to develop compelling arguments that support veterans’ cases.

- Stay informed about updates in VA regulations, disability law, and relevant medical issues.

- Ensure accuracy and completeness in all research and written materials.



Qualifications:

- Demonstrated track record of conducting thorough research and producing clear, well-written analyses based on that research.

- Prior experience in legal research, claims analysis, or paralegal work is preferred.

- Strong analytical and research skills with the ability to comprehend complex information.

- Excellent writing and communication skills, with the ability to present findings persuasively.

- Self-motivated and detail-oriented, with a problem-solving mindset.

- Familiarity with medical terminology or VA disability claims is a plus.



Why Join Rocket Benefits?

- Competitive salary.

- Opportunity to make a direct, positive impact on the lives of U.S. veterans and their families.

- Collaborative and mission-driven work culture.

- Room for growth and career development in a rapidly expanding company.



How to Apply:

If you’re ready to bring your research and analytical skills to a team that values dedication and impact, we’d love to hear from you. Please submit your resume and include “Yippee” in your application title.

title: Midweight E?commerce & CRM Specialist (D2C)
description:
Location: Remote (with overlap to UK business hours)

Seniority: Midweight (3+ years D2C e?commerce experience)

Reports to: Head of Growth

Works closely with: Developer, Creative Team, CRM Specialist, Ops/Fulfilment, Customer Service



About STYRKR



At STYRKR, we’re on a mission to make high-quality D2C e?commerce experiences simple, engaging, and revenue-driving. We’re looking for a hands-on midweight e?commerce specialist who loves rolling up their sleeves and ensuring everything runs smoothly—from Shopify storefronts to subscription programs, on-site merchandising, CRO experiments, and
 marketing coordination.



You’ll partner closely with the Head of Growth on strategy, collaborate with our Creative Team for eye-catching assets, and work with a CRM Specialist to make sure every campaign lands perfectly.



What You’ll Be Doing



As our e?commerce specialist, you’ll be the go-to person for keeping our UK/EU storefronts and subscription operations in top shape. Your responsibilities will include:



1. Shopify Trading & Site Operations

Manage daily Shopify admin: products, collections, tags, content updates, offers, and QA.

Keep basket mechanics and promotions running smoothly (upsells, free delivery thresholds, free gifts, ATC messaging).

Ensure multi-region consistency and end-to-end QA across UK, EU, and Channel Islands.



2. Recharge Subscriptions & Retention

Set up and maintain subscription plans.

Manage legacy plans, swap collections, and subscription UX components.

Implement churn prevention tools and subscription-linked loyalty rewards.



3. Build Your Own Bundle (BYOB)

Maintain live and legacy BYOB products/pages.

Handle tiered discount logic, free gifts, and collection architecture.

Coordinate with Ops and Dev to ensure fulfilment logic works flawlessly.



4. CRO Testing & On-Site Experimentation

Run and QA A/B tests across all storefronts.

Track results, document learnings, and recommend next steps.



5. CRM Coordination & Creative Briefing (Klaviyo)

Manage the campaign workflow: brief ? creative ? review ? build ? QA ? schedule.

Maintain promotional and retention flow calendars.

Report insights to Head of Growth with clarity and actionable recommendations.



6. Cross-Functional Delivery & Issue Resolution

Triaging operational or customer service issues impacting conversion.

Coordinate with internal teams and external vendors.

Build SOPs and checklists to prevent recurring issues.



Who We’re Looking For



Must-haves:

- 3+ years hands-on D2C e?commerce experience (operator level).

- Strong Shopify admin skills (themes, products, promotions, collections, tagging).

- Recharge experience (subscriptions, retention, swaps).

- Klaviyo coordination experience (campaigns, flows, QA).

- Ability to brief and coordinate a creative team effectively.

- Exceptional QA mindset and attention to detail.



Nice-to-haves:

- CRO testing experience.

- Familiarity with reviews/loyalty platforms.

- Exposure to fulfilment or warehouse systems.

- Experience with TikTok Shop operations.

- Comfortable working from Figma and collaborating with developers.



What Success Looks Like:

- Site updates shipped quickly and accurately across regions.

- Subscription programs run smoothly with low friction and minimal CS tickets.

- Campaigns and flows in Klaviyo run reliably and contribute to measurable revenue.

- BYOB operations remain stable and continuously improved.

- Weekly clarity for Head of Growth: what shipped, what’s next, what’s blocked, and what’s driving revenue.



What We Offer:

- Fully remote role with flexibility and UK overlap (min. 3–4 hours/day).

- Opportunity to work with a fast-moving, collaborative D2C team.

- Exposure to multi-region e?commerce operations, subscription programs, and growth marketing.

- Real ownership of your work, your ideas, execution, and impact matter.

title: Chartered Accountant
description:
Senior Chartered Accountant - Certified Public Accountant (100% Remote – EST focus)



Are you a strategic financial leader who thrives on precision? We are seeking a high-caliber Chartered Accountant / CPA to serve as the right hand to our CFO. This isn’t just a "numbers" role—it’s an opportunity to drive global financial strategy, optimize international treasury, and provide the insights that fuel our growth.

If you are a post-qualified professional who excels in a high-accountability, remote environment and is comfortable working fixed Eastern Standard Time (EST) hours, we want to hear from you.



Why This Role?



Strategic Impact: Influence our long-term financial roadmap and lead the way in GAAP/IFRS compliance for a global organization.



Executive Visibility: Work directly with the CFO and Board of Directors, delivering the KPI dashboards that shape executive decisions.



Career Growth: Lead and mentor a dedicated finance team while managing high-level tax planning and treasury operations.



What You’ll Do



Financial Leadership: Develop financial strategies, manage annual budgets, and oversee rolling forecasts to ensure stability.



Reporting Excellence: Prepare monthly management accounts and Board-level reports with 100?curacy and timeliness.



Treasury & Compliance: Optimize working capital, manage global banking relationships, and lead all internal/external audits.



Tea
ntorship: Supervise and develop the finance team, fostering a culture of excellence and integrity.



Who You Are



The Professional: You hold a CPA, CA, ACA, or equivalent designation with at least 5 years of post-qualification experience in a senior role.



The Expert: You have advanced knowledge of GAAP/IFRS and are a power user of ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, or NetSuite) and Excel.



The Strategist: You possess a "big picture" mindset while maintaining meticulous attention to detail.



The Remote Pro: You thrive in high-transparency environments and are comfortable with performance monitoring tools (e.g., Time Doctor).



Requirements at a Glance



Education: Professional Accounting Designation (CPA/CA/ACA) is required.



Work Schedule: Fixed EST Shift (Night Shift in the Philippines). Please ensure you are comfortable with this schedule.



Technical Specs (Home Office):



Processor: Core i7 (or equivalent) or better.



Memory: 16GB RAM minimum.



Internet: 20Mbps+ stable fiber connection with a backup power/internet solution.



Soft Skills: Exceptional English communication skills, high integrity, and the ability to work independently.

title: Full-Stack Web Designer (Design-First)
description:
We’re hiring a design-first full-stack web designer to build high-converting landing pages, quiz flows/pages, and other funnel pages as needed.



This is part-time or project-based (paid per project or hourly). If it’s a great fit, there’s ongoing work.



What you’ll do

* Design + build landing pages (conversion-focused, modern, fast)

* Design + build quiz pages / multi-step flows (UI/UX, logic-friendly layout)

* Create reusable sections/components so we can iterate quickly

* Ensure pages are responsive, pixel-clean, and performance-optimized

* Collaborate with us on iterations based on feedback and data



What we care about (read this)

Design quality is the #1 priority.?We’re not looking for “acceptable” or “pretty good.” We want someone who consistently produces excellent design and can execute it in code.

If your work looks like templates, generic themes, or “close enough,” this won’t be a fit.



Requirements

* Strong portfolio showing landing pages / funnels / marketing pages you designed

* Ability to turn design into a real, working page (not just Figma)

* Great eye for: typography, spacing, hierarchy, color, layout, components

* Solid front-end implementation skills (responsive, accessibility basics, clean CSS)

* Familiar with modern web stacks (any of these is fine):

    * Webflow / Framer (if you can build fast and clean)

    * React / Next.js

    * HTML/CSS/JS + a modern component system

* Can work quickly without sacrificing polish



Nice to have

* CRO experience (you’ve designed for conversion, not just aesthetics)

* Quiz / form flow experience (Typeform-style UX, multi-step funnels)

* Motion / micro-interactions (subtle, tasteful)

* Copy sense (you can improve layout + clarity, suggest better structure)



How to apply (required)

Send:

1. Portfolio link (must include landing pages you designed)

2. 2–3 examples you’re most proud of + what you did (design, build, both)

title: Order Project Manager
description:
Do you thrive on turning big ideas into beautifully crafted realities? Are you the kind of person who gets excited by timelines, thrives under pressure, and loves making every detail count? At Styled Settings, we’re not just designing kitchen products—we’re creating experiences that inspire joy and elevate everyday life. 



As our Order Project Manager, you’ll be at the heart of it all, orchestrating the journey to ensure products are delivered as designed and at great quality.



Reporting to our Head of Supply Chain, this is your chance to lead critical projects end-to-end, working across time zones with manufacturing partners in China and creative teams in the USA. You’ll own every aspect of the process: tracking orders with Gantt charts, aligning creative artwork (from our creative team) with production requirements, managing quality control (coordinating inspections), and delivering updates that keep leadership in the loop. Whether it’s tackling inspection schedules, resolving production risks, or fine-tuning workflows, your impact will be visible in every Styled Settings product that reaches a customer’s hands.



We’re looking for a master multitasker who can juggle complexity with grace, a proactive problem-solver who thinks three steps ahead, and a detail-driven communicator who thrives on collaboration. You don’t need to be an expert in kitchenware—just someone who loves beautiful products, values quality, and has the drive to see projects through to perfection.



Who Should Apply? (Apply Here: 

 • Project Management Pros: If you’re skilled with tools like Gantt charts, ClickUp, and Google Sheets, and you have 3+ years of experience managing complex projects, we want you.

 • Global Collaborators: If you’ve worked with international vendors (bonus points for experience in China) and excel at coordinating across time zones, this is your opportunity to shine.

 • Detail-Oriented Leaders: If you’re laser-focused on tracking every dependency, milestone, and risk while delivering top-notch updates to leadership, this role is made for you.

 • Problem-Solving Champions: If you thrive on anticipating challenges and crafting solutions that keep projects on track, you’ll love this role.

 • Lovers of Design and Quality: If you appreciate the beauty of a well-designed product and the satisfaction of delivering exceptional quality, you’ll fit right in.



Join Styled Settings and become part of a company that’s passionate about creating products that make life more beautiful—and a workplace where your contributions truly matter. Let’s create something extraordinary together!

interests, demographics, personality, seasonality, and more.

Yibby’s recommendation engine is powered by a curated gift database and supported through

affiliate partnerships with Amazon, Target, Etsy, TikTok Shop, and others. Our mission is to

make gift-giving joyful, personal, and effortless.



About the Role

We’re hiring a Product Manager who will also serve as Yibby’s “Gift Master” — the human

curator responsible for making sure Yibby recommends amazing, high-quality gifts.

This is a hands-on role focused on building and maintaining the universe of gifts that Yibby can

recommend. You will ensure that every item in our system is:

? A genuinely great gift

? High-quality

? In-stock and up to date

? Seasonally appropriate

? Eligible for strong affiliate commissions

? Matched to the correct recipient profiles and occasions



Your Responsibilities Include

1. Curating the Gift Catalog

? Evaluating products across major affiliate marketplaces

? Determining which items make excellent gifts (vs. generic products)

? Tagging and categorizing gifts by age, interests, hobbies, personality, and special

occasions

? Prioritizing high-commission, high-delight items



2. Optimizing Based on Business Goals

? Filtering out low-commission, low-quality, or unavailable items

? Tracking seasonal trends and upcoming holidays

? Identifying trending items, viral products, and new releases

3. Maintaining the Gift Database

? Creating structured attributes and metadata for each product

? Improving the taxonomy of categories, tags, and matching logic

? Ensuring the recommendation engine has the right inputs to select great gifts

4. Reviewing Gift Content

You will collaborate with Yibby’s content team responsible for producing:

? Product photos

? Lifestyle images

? Short videos

? AI-generated UGC or demos

Your role is to ensure the content is accurate, appealing, and suited to the gifting

scenario.

5. Working with Product & Engineering

? Presenting new gift categories or seasonal collections

? Partnering with engineering to refine the recommendation engine

? Translating product ideas into structured requirements or data needs

Yibby currently does not have a dedicated UX designer, so you will sometimes use AI

tools or simple mockups to visualize flows, data structures, or tagging systems.



What We’re Looking For

? Experience in product management, e-commerce, merchandising, or similar

? Strong research skills and attention to detail

? An instinct for what makes a great gift

? Excellent written English and communication skills

? Comfort using AI tools for research or content creation

? Highly organized with the ability to work independently



Nice to Have

? Experience with affiliate programs (Amazon Associates, TikTok Shop, etc.)

? Background in e-commerce catalog management

? Knowledge of gifting behavior, consumer trends, or product psychology



? Experience reviewing or creating product photos or short-form content

? Interest in recommendation systems or personalization engines



Click here to apply:

title: Product Manager (Early-Stage Digital Platform)
description:
Company: BriarBear

Location: Fully Remote (Philippines)

Schedule: Graveyard Shift (Start time between 6:00 PM - 8:00 PM PH Time)

Employment Type: Full-time



About this Role

We are building a new digital platform that is already in active development and preparing for

launch. We are looking for a Product Manager to help take the product from “working” to

excellent—with a strong focus on usability, clarity, quality, and early user adoption.

This role will work closely under a senior Product Manager and alongside a small, focused team

to refine features, improve user flows, ensure high product quality, and support launch

readiness.

While this position is posted as Product Manager, it is best described as a Product Specialist /

Associate Product Manager role. You will be deeply hands-on with the product and involved in

day-to-day execution, testing, iteration, and operational support.

This is an ideal role for someone who wants to own a real product, learn how early-stage

platforms are shaped, and contribute directly to something that will be used by real customers.



What You’ll Be Working On

? A web-based platform with:

? User accounts and profiles

? Core workflows that must be simple and intuitive

? A clear value proposition for multiple user types

? A product that must be:

? Easy to understand on first use

? Reliable and polished at launch

? Ready to support marketing and growth efforts



You will help ensure the product is something people actually want to sign up for and use.



Key Responsibilities

1. Product Research & Insight Gathering

? Research comparable platforms and products in related spaces

? Identify:



? Common feature patterns

? Best practices

? Gaps and opportunities

? Share concise insights and recommendations with the Product Manager and team

2. Product Definition & Feature Support

? Assist in defining and refining:

? Core features

? MVP scope

? User flows and edge cases

? Help translate ideas into:

? Clear requirements

? Actionable tasks for development

? Keep the team aligned on priorities and next steps

3. QA & Product Quality Ownership

? Thoroughly test features as they are built

? Identify bugs, UX issues, and unclear flows

? Ensure:

? Key user actions work smoothly

? The product feels professional and trustworthy

? Act as the primary QA owner before features are released

4. Generalist Product & Operations Support

Because this is a small, early-stage team, you will also:

? Help respond to user questions or issues via

? Assist with basic customer support and troubleshooting

? Support operational tasks related to the platform

? Provide feedback on marketing assets, onboarding flows, and landing pages as

needed

This is a hands-on role where flexibility and ownership matter.

5. Cross-Functional Collaboration

? Work daily with:

? A Product Manager

? A Developer

? A Marketing teammate

? Ensure the product supports marketing and growth initiatives

? Provide feedback and insights that improve conversion and user experience



Who This Role is For

? A strong generalist who enjoys switching between research, testing, and execution

? Someone detail-oriented who cares deeply about product quality

? A problem-solver who doesn’t need rigid structure to be effective

? Someone comfortable working closely with a small team and leadership



Required Skills & Experience

? Strong written and spoken English

? Ability to clearly document and explain product issues and ideas

? Comfort working with developers and testing live products

? High attention to detail and organization

? Willingness to handle customer-facing and operational tasks

? Ability to work graveyard shift aligned with US-based teams



Nice to Have (But Not Required)

? Experience with early-stage products or startups

? Familiarity with product tools (Notion, Figma, Jira, Linear, etc.)

? Experience supporting or QA-ing web platforms

? Interest in digital platforms, marketplaces, or SaaS products



Why This Role Matters

This product is approaching launch, and the decisions made now will directly affect:

? User trust

? Signup rates

? Early growth and retention

You’ll be close to the product every day and will play a meaningful role in shaping how it

performs in the real world.



Internal Note on Title

? External Title: Product Manager

? Internal Role: Product Specialist / Associate Product Manager



Click here to apply:

title: Senior eMoney Financial Planning Specialist
description:
Senior eMoney Financial Planning Specialist



Company: Ensign Partners

Location: Remote (Philippines)

Schedule: Full-time, Long-term role



Ensign Partners brings simplicity to complexity by integrating financial, tax, insurance, and advisory expertise under one roof. Our clients receive one coordinated plan, one trusted team, and a clear path forward.

We are a values-driven firm built on professionalism, trust, and long-term relationships. Every role at Ensign matters. We believe exceptional client experiences are created through consistency, accuracy, and people who take pride in doing things the right way. We are growing and looking for a highly experienced financial planning professional to join our team.



This is not an entry-level role.



We are only looking for candidates with significant, hands-on experience using eMoney.



Position Overview

We are hiring a Senior Financial Planning Specialist with deep expertise in eMoney Advisor to support our financial planning team.  Your primary responsibility will be building, maintaining, and stress-testing comprehensive financial plans inside eMoney. You will work directly with our advisors to ensure every plan is accurate, strategic, and presentation-ready.



You must already be highly proficient in eMoney. Training will not be provided on basic functionality.



This position is ideal for someone who values structure, ownership, precision, and being a trusted contributor within a professional team environment.



Key Responsibilities:

-Build comprehensive financial plans in eMoney from advisor notes and client data

-Update and maintain existing client plans

-Model advanced scenarios:

---Retirement projections

---Tax strategies

---Insurance planning

---Estate planning assumptions

---Business exit scenarios

---Social Security optimization

---Cash flow planning

-Run Monte Carlo simulations and interpret results

-Identify inconsistencies or planning gaps

-Prepare client-ready reports and presentation materials

-Assist advisors in preparing for client review meetings

-Maintain clean and organized client data within eMoney

-Collaborate with tea
mbers via Zoom and project management tools



Required Qualifications:

-Hands-on experience using eMoney Advisor

-Experience working with a U.S.-based financial advisory or wealth management firm

-Strong understanding of:

---Retirement income planning

---Tax-efficient distribution strategies

---Insurance planning

---Investment accounts and asset allocation

---Social Security strategies

-Advanced Excel skills

-Excellent written and verbal English

-High attention to detail



What Success Looks Like:

-Advisors trust your planning work without rechecking numbers

-Plans are clean, accurate, and strategically thoughtful

-You proactively catch issues before meetings

-You think like a planner, not just a data-entry person



Core Values You Embody at Ensign Partners:

-Enjoy the Journey – You bring steadiness, positivity, and professionalism to your work.

-Move the Cheese – You anticipate needs and adapt as processes evolve.

-Be Your Best – You care deeply about accuracy, presentation, and follow-through.

-Aim to Win – You take ownership and see tasks through without constant oversight.

-Raise the Bar – High standards come naturally to you.

-Keep Your Word – You do what you say you will do, every time.

-Stronger as One – You collaborate well and understand your role supports the whole team.



Why Join Ensign Partners, Ensign Partners is intentional about building a workplace where people are trusted, supported, and held to high standards. This role is essential to delivering the consistent, high-quality experience our commercial clients expect.



Joining Ensign means:

-Being part of a mission-driven, people-first firm

-Working in a professional, organized, and collaborative environment

-Having clear expectations and the ability to take pride in your work

-Contributing meaningfully to a growing and respected organization

-If you are detail-driven, experienced in commercial insurance servicing, and committed to delivering accurate and responsive client support, we encourage you to apply and become part of the Ensign Partners team.



IMPORTANT: Instructions When Applying



Please follow these instructions carefully. They exist to filter serious candidates.



1. Do NOT
 us directly.

If you
 us outside of this job post, you will be automatically disqualified.



2. Your subject line must be:

eMoney Expert



3. In your application, include ALL of the following:

-Describe your experience using eMoney in detail.

-Explain a complex financial planning scenario you have built inside eMoney.

-Provide examples of U.S.-based firms you’ve worked with.

-Your English proficiency level



PAY WILL DEPEND ON LEVEL OF EXPERIENCE.

Work hours will be full time US (Arizona-based) business hours.

title: Real Estate Operations Lead
description:
PropZap is building a next-generation real estate platform designed to expand across North America.



We are pioneers in how modern real estate companies should be built — powered by strong systems, smart technology, data-driven decision-making, and operational efficiency.



We’re building with intention.

With structure.

With technology at the core.

With performance that compounds over time.



Our goal is to create a brand that sets the standard. A company that leads. A platform built to scale intelligently and sustainably across markets.



And for the right person, this isn’t just a job inside that company.



It’s a front-row seat to its growth.



--------------------------------------------------------------



Role: Real Estate Operations Lead

Location: Philippines (Remote)

Shift: Night Shift — Canada MST

Role Type: Full-Time, Long-Term

Compensation: Top-tier salary + performance bonuses + growth-based increases



We believe strong operators deserve strong compensation. If you take ownership and help us grow confidently, you will be rewarded accordingly.



--------------------------------------------------------------



Why This Role Is a Big Opportunity



This isn’t just about processing files.



This is about helping build a company that scales smoothly.



As PropZap expands into new markets and increases transaction volume, you’ll be part of strengthening the operational backbone that allows that growth to happen confidently.



As we grow, your scope grows.



More responsibility.

More visibility.

More leadership potential over time.



If you’re looking for long-term stability and upward movement instead of hopping between short-term roles, this is that kind of opportunity.



--------------------------------------------------------------



Your Core Responsibilities:

You will own and lead the backend operations that power our agents and client experience.

Full Real Estate Operations Leadership (Hands-On)



You will personally execute and oversee:

- Transaction and listing coordination

• Contract preparation, amendments, compliance, and document management

• MLS drafts, uploads, and accuracy checks

• FINTRAC, signatures, deadlines, and audit-ready files

• CRM hygiene, pipelines, task boards, and workflow tracking

• Calendar management, reminders, and operational follow-ups



You will ensure nothing slips, nothing is messy, and everything is executed with precision.



Listing & Marketing Operations:

- Coordinate photos, drone, measurements, floorplans, and virtual tours

• Manage listing timelines, deliverables, and launch readiness

• Prepare polished listing descriptions and marketing instructions

• Oversee signs, lockboxes, open houses, and showing instructions

• Maintain organized listing folders, media libraries, and assets

• Coordinate with marketing vendors and internal resources



Agent & Client Backend Support:

- Act as the primary operational support behind agents

• Communicate professionally with clients, lawyers, vendors, and partners

• Track conditions, deadlines, and next steps

• Ensure agents can focus on selling while operations run flawlessly



Systems, Process, and Scale:

- Maintain clean, reliable, and scalable operational systems

• Identify inefficiencies and proactively improve workflows

• Document processes and help build SOPs as the business evolves

• Support leadership with operational insights and execution

• Assist in hiring, training, and onboarding future operations tea
mbers



Who This Role Is Great For



Someone who enjoys ownership.



Someone who likes being the person everything runs through smoothly.



Someone who wants to grow inside a scaling company.



• Experience in real estate operations (U.S. or Canadian experience is a plus)

• Organized and detail-oriented

• Comfortable managing multiple files at once

• Clear and professional communicator

• Proactive and solutions-focused

• Interested in long-term growth



Highly Ideal:

• Experience mentoring others

• Experience improving workflows

• Experience in fast-growing environments



--------------------------------------------------------------



What You Gain



• Above-market compensation

• Performance bonuses tied to impact

• Long-term stability inside a scaling company

• Direct exposure to leadership

• A clear path toward greater operational responsibility

• A role that expands as the company expands



Imagine being two or three years into a company that has expanded across markets — and knowing you helped build the operational backbone that supported that growth.



That’s what this role offers.



--------------------------------------------------------------



How to Apply



Send the following to: 



• Resume

• Portfolio (if available)

• 2-minute intro video introducing yourself

• Summary of your real estate operations experience

• Expected monthly salary (PHP)

• Internet speed test link

• Any DISC test result



Subject Line: Real Estate Operations Lead – Your Name



If you’re ready to grow with a company that’s expanding — and build something meaningful behind the scenes — we’d love to hear from you.

title: Marketing Manager
description:
PropZap — Building the Future of Real Estate in North America



PropZap is building a next-generation real estate platform designed to expand across North America.



We are pioneers in how modern real estate companies should be built — powered by strong systems, smart technology, data-driven decision-making, and operational efficiency.



We’re building with intention.

With structure.

With technology at the core.

With performance that compounds over time.



Our goal is to create a brand that sets the standard. A company that leads. A platform built to scale intelligently and sustainably across markets.



And for the right person, this isn’t just a job inside that company.

Role: Marketing Manager

We believe great people build great companies. If you can truly take ownership of growth and deliver measurable results, we are willing to offer premium compensation. Strong operators who scale performance will be rewarded at the top end of the market.

Why This Role Is a Big Opportunity for You

This role has a clear path toward future Head of Marketing for someone who wants to build long term — not just manage campaigns quarter to quarter.



You’ll be building something that compounds — and your career compounds with it.



--------------------------------------------------------------



What You’ll Be Doing



Meta Ads Leadership (Most Critical)

• Plan creative angles, hooks, and positioning

• Write high-converting direct-response ad copy

)

Analytics, Tracking & Attribution

• Maintain clear attribution from click to handoff

• Analyze full-funnel performance

• Identify bottlenecks and recommend improvements

• Document A/B tests and build repeatable optimization frameworks



Marketing Innovation & Leadership

• Proactively suggest growth ideas

• Stay updated on new tools, platforms, and strategies

• Serve as the internal expert for performance marketing

• Guide leadership decisions with data-backed insights



--------------------------------------------------------------



Who This Is Perfect For



This role is ideal for someone who wants stability with upside.



Someone who wants to stop freelancing unpredictably and build something consistent.

Someone who enjoys ownership and wants their work to matter.



• Proven hands-on experience managing significant Meta Ads budgets

• Strong understanding of direct-response marketing

• Comfortable with analytics and attribution

• Strong copywriting and creative strategy skills

• Structured and disciplined with testing



Highly Ideal:

• Real estate marketing experience

• Experience generating buyer or seller leads

• Experience promoting brokerages



Bonus If You Have:

• SEO experience

• Google Ads experience

• AI tools and automation knowledge

• Zapier or workflow automation experience



--------------------------------------------------------------



What You’ll Gain



• Premium compensation aligned with performance

• Long-term stability inside a scaling company

• Direct collaboration with founders

• Real ownership and influence over strategy

• Clear path toward leadership

• The ability to grow as the company grows



Imagine being two or three years into a company that has expanded across multiple markets — and knowing you helped build the engine that powered that expansion.



That’s the opportunity here.



We’re building something designed to grow.



If you want your career to grow with it, we’d love to meet you.



--------------------------------------------------------------



How to Apply



Send the following to: 



• Resume (required)

• Portfolio (required)

• Case studies or performance summaries of Meta campaigns (required)

• Summary of budgets managed (required)

• Intro video (preferred)

• Expected monthly compensation (required)

• Internet speed test link (required)

• Any DISC test result (if available)



Subject Line: Performance Marketing Manager – Your Name



If you’re ready to stop hopping and start building something that scales — this might be the role you’ve been waiting for.

title: Social Media Manager
description:
PropZap is building a next-generation real estate platform designed to expand across North America.



We are pioneers in how modern real estate companies should be built — powered by strong systems, smart technology, data-driven decision-making, and operational efficiency.



We’re not following outdated playbooks.



We’re building with intention.

With structure.

With technology at the core.

With performance that compounds over time.



Our goal is to create a recognizable brand across markets — one that agents are proud to join, clients trust, and competitors respect.



And to build a brand at that level, we need someone who wants to help shape how we show up to the world.



--------------------------------------------------------------



Role: Social Media Manager

Location: Remote

Shift: Aligned with Canada MST

Role Type: Any

Compensation: 50,000 PHP+



We believe strong creatives deserve strong compensation. For someone who can truly take ownership of our brand presence and elevate how we show up online, we are willing to pay premium for quality and consistency.



--------------------------------------------------------------



Why This Role Is a Big Opportunity for You



If you’ve worked in social media roles where you were just “the poster”…

If you’ve had ideas that never got approved…

If you’ve wanted more ownership but were boxed into small tasks…



This is different.



Here, you help build the brand.



You won’t just execute content — you’ll influence how an expanding company is perceived across markets.



As we grow, our visibility grows.

As our visibility grows, your creative influence grows with it.



This role evolves as the company scales. What starts as full ownership of social content can expand into larger brand leadership opportunities over time.



If you’re looking for stability, long-term growth, and a place where your creativity actually matters — this is that kind of role.



--------------------------------------------------------------



What You’ll Be Doing



This is a brand-building role with full content ownership.



You’ll handle content from ideation to execution — including editing, graphic design, marketing materials, feature sheets, publishing, inbox management, and performance tracking.



There will be some short-form reels, but this role is not heavily focused on daily short-form volume. Quality and positioning matter more than quantity.



--------------------------------------------------------------



Your Core Responsibilities



Full-Cycle Content Ownership



Content Ideation & Strategy

• Develop BGC-focused content aligned with brand positioning

• Generate original ideas that educate, attract, and convert

• Plan content calendars and campaign themes



Content Production & Editing

• Edit short-form video when required (not high-volume)

• Support long-form video editing when needed

• Create graphics, carousels, thumbnails, and branded visuals

• Design professional feature sheets

title: Customer Service Manager (DTC eCommerce)
description:
FULL-TIME, LONG-TERM, EXCLUSIVE ROLE ONLY

No part-time work, agency setups, or side commitments.



We are a fast-growing supplement brand looking for a Customer Support Manager who is genuinely obsessed with efficiency and continuous improvement. This is not a role for someone who just wants to maintain the status quo or follow a manual. We need a leader who looks at a process and immediately thinks of three ways to make it faster, smarter, and better.



You will be responsible for building and scaling our support operations. We want someone who takes deep pride in ownership, turns chaos into systems, and ensures our customer experience is a competitive advantage. If you are a builder who thrives on fixing broken workflows and implementing high-level strategy, we want to talk to you.



What You Will Do



* Optimize & Implement: You will be the architect of our CS roadmap, constantly auditing and improving systems to ensure we stay ahead of our growth.

* System Ownership: Beyond just managing tickets, you will create, improve, and maintain our entire library of SOPs, macros, and internal documentation.

* Problem Identification: You will proactively identify patterns in customer friction and work across departments to solve issues at the root.

* Tech Stack Management: Take full ownership of our tools (Gorgias, Shopify, etc.), ensuring all automations, rules, and integrations are running at peak performance.

* Accountability: Own the department KPIs (CSAT, Resolution Time, Efficiency) and use data to drive every decision you make.

* Hiring: Hire, promote, and remove underperformers to maintain a high-output, accountable team culture

* Lead by Example: Set the standard for communication and problem-solving, eventually leading the hiring and training as the team scales.



Qualifications



* 5+ years leading customer service teams of 15+ in a fast-paced eCommerce environment

* Obsession with Improvement: You must be able to demonstrate how you have personally improved a system, workflow, or department in a previous role.

* Shopify Expert: Strong hands-on experience with Shopify and its ecosystem of apps.

* Tool Mastery: Advanced proficiency in Gorgias, Zendesk, or similar platforms, specifically in building out automations and complex workflows.

* Strategic Thinker: Ability to translate raw data into actionable process changes.

* Communication: Exceptional written and verbal English with the ability to lead with authority and clarity.

* Commitment: Willingness to commit full-time and exclusively to our brand for the long term.



This Role is Not for You If:



* You view this as "just a job" rather than something you take personal pride in building

* You need step-by-step direction and shy away from strategic decisions

* You're uncomfortable holding people accountable or addressing underperformance

* You present problems instead of solving them

* You want a comfortable, predictable 9-to-5 job



Click here to apply : 
/jobs/
customer-support-manager

title: Media Buyer Meta Strategist
description:
Our company creates premium acrylic staging systems used by caterers, designers, and event professionals operating at a higher standard. Our products appear in private rooms, elevated events, and high-end hospitality environments.

Every decision inside the company is tied to presentation, precision, and performance. Now we’re expanding our marketing engine and we’re looking for a Meta strategist, not a technician.



If your experience is limited to boosted posts, YouTube tutorials, or “ran ads once for a small business,” this role isn't for you.



We’re looking for someone who understands:



° Meta’s Andromeda ecosystem

° How to protect signal quality

° How to design creative testing matrices

° How to scale responsibly

° How to make decisions with incomplete attribution

° How creative, audience structure, and platform signals interact to produce sales

° Your decisions will directly influence revenue and cost structure.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:



° You will own performance — not tasks. Your responsibilities include:

° Campaign Strategy & Build

  - Design, launch, and optimize campaigns under the Andromeda framework

  - Maintain simplified structure with clear signal flow

  - Manage catalog-based and SKU-level performance



° Creative Testing & Collaboration

  - Build creative testing matrices (hooks, angles, formats)

  - Brief UGC creators and editors with data-driven insights

  - Identify creative fatigue, thumbstop failures, and winners early



° Analytics & Revenue Diagnostics

  - Monitor CPM, CTR, CPC, ROAS, AOV, MER

  - Diagnose signal loss, attribution drops, pixel/CAPI inconsistencies

  - Run weekly revenue diagnostics to guide spend allocation



° Scaling & Risk Management

  - Execute scaling without disrupting MER

  - Plan and manage ramp-up/ramp-down cycles

  - Respond quickly to auction shifts, CPM spikes, and performance drops



° Continuous Improvement

  - Stay updated on Meta auction changes and Andromeda updates

  - Own process development for creative ? data ? iteration loops



QUALIFICATIONS 



° 2+ years Meta Ads management

° Managed ?300,000+/month in ad spend (or equivalent across accounts)

° Hands-on experience with Andromeda structure:

   - signal clarity

   - broad structure

   - creative-led systems

   - audience consolidation

° Proven ability to improve MER, not just platform ROAS

° Experience testing 20+ creatives/month

° Strong analytical ability: blended metrics, attribution logic, funnel interpretation

° Ability to work independently with strategic judgment



BONUS SKILLS



° Experience in lifestyle, design, hospitality, or luxury categories

° Knowledge of UGC-style ads and creator management

° Familiarity with TikTok Ads or Google Ads

° Understanding of premium product positioning and editorial aesthetics



COMPENSATION



° 30,000–50,000/month

° Performance bonuses

° Long-term, stable position

° Remote, results-driven culture

title: Senior eCommerce Accountant (120K to 150K)
description:
SENIOR ECOMMERCE ACCOUNTANT



Location: Philippines

Reports to: Head of Accounting

Employment Type: Full-Time Independent Contractor, Remote



At Firm Five, we modernize bookkeeping and fractional controllership for fast-growing businesses. We serve eCommerce and service-based companies with $2M–$50M in annual revenue across Canada and the U.S., helping founders move fro
ssy numbers to clear, decision-ready financials.



We don’t do tax work.

We do take ownership of accounting, systems, and process improvements—and we do it well.



POSITION SUMMARY



We are hiring a Senior eCommerce Accountant to own the monthly accounting and reporting for a portfolio of U.S. and Canadian eCommerce brands.



You will be responsible for end-to-end monthly close in QuickBooks Online or Xero for clients selling on Shopify, Amazon, Walmart, Etsy, and similar platforms. This is a senior role suited for someone who can work independently, spot issues before they become problems, and communicate clearly with both internal tea
mbers and clients.



COMPENSATION



PHP 120,000–150,000 per month, based on experience and skill level

Performance-based increases and growth opportunities available



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



Set up and optimize eCommerce-friendly charts of accounts (revenue, payment processors, COGS, inventory)



Perform monthly reconciliations for banks, credit cards, and platforms such as:



Shopify Payments, Stripe, PayPal



Amazon, Walmart, and other marketplace settlements



Maintain clean transaction categorization aligned with eCommerce activity



Prepare monthly P&L, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow Statements



Record accruals, inventory adjustments, landed costs, and COGS entries



Track sales tax liabilities (no tax filing required)



Handle opening balances and catch-up bookkeeping when onboarding new clients



Identify and resolve discrepancies in revenue, payouts, returns, fees, and inventory



Communicate clearly and professionally with clients via Slack and
 (English)



Use or learn tools such as A2X, Webgility, or similar eCommerce accounting software



QUALIFICATIONS



Bachelor’s degree in Accounting



CPA designation (Philippines required)



3–5+ years of full-cycle accounting experience, ideally within a US , Canadian or AUS firm environment



Hands-on experience with eCommerce or inventory accounting and marketplace reconciliations



Experience with QBO or Xero (required)



Familiarity with Shopify, Amazon Seller Central, A2X, Webgility, or similar tools is a strong asset



Prior experience supporting U.S., Canadian, or Australian clients preferred



CHARACTERISTICS FOR SUCCESS



Comfortable owning monthly close with minimal supervision



Detail-oriented but able to see the bigger picture



Strong written and spoken English communication skills



Organized, reliable, and deadline-driven



Proactive, solution-focused mindset



Stable internet connection, dedicated workstation, and backup power supply



WHY YOU’LL LIKE WORKING WITH FIRM FIVE



Competitive compensation paid bi-weekly via Wise



Fully remote role with flexible schedule

(Core overlap: 9:00 AM–1:00 PM EST + 4 flexible hours)



Paid holidays, vacation, and sick/personal days



Early employee incentive plan tied to company growth



Supportive, low-drama team environment



No screen tracking or time-monitoring software



Clear processes, expectations, and room to grow



IMPORTANT NOTE



This role is a full-time independent contractor position.

Government-mandated PH benefits are not provided; however, Firm Five offers paid time off and paid holidays as part of the engagement.



OUR COMMITMENT TO EQUITY & INCLUSION



Firm Five is committed to building a respectful, inclusive, and supportive workplace. We value diversity of background and perspective and are dedicated to creating an environment where people can do great work and grow their careers.

 and phone

*Cross-Team Collaboration

Who We’re Looking For:

- Experience with QuickBooks Online and bookkeeping best practices

- Preferred experience with CRMs and ERP systems

- Strong communication and organizational skills

- Experience working with project management tools like 

- Tech-Savvy and an analytical mindset with the ability to troubleshoot system/data issues

Requirements:

title: HR Generalist
description:
Join Our Remote Accounting Team!

We are a fast-growing, boutique outsourced accounting firm supporting high-growth e-commerce and service-based businesses. We’ve been behind the scenes helping some of the most exciting DTC brands scale with confidence through expert bookkeeping, financial systems, and strategic insights.



We are a remote-first company with a collaborative, client-first culture — and we’re looking for exceptional talent to grow with us.



About the Role

We are looking for a hands-on HR Generalist who thrives in a startup environment and is excited to help build the HR function from the ground up. This role goes beyond day-to-day HR support — you will play a key role in setting up foundational HR processes, shaping people practices, and supporting the growth of our teams as the company scales.



As a young and fast-growing company, growth opportunities are significant. You will have the chance to help build teams, influence culture, and design systems that will evolve alongside the business. While the team currently operates in a distributed setup, the role is designed to support both remote and future on-site tea
mbers.



You will work closely with the HR and Talent Acquisition Manager to establish strong HR frameworks, ensure compliance, and deliver a positive tea
mber experience across the U.S. and the Philippines.



Your Responsibilities

** Recruitment & Talent Acquisition

- Manage full-cycle recruitment especially for finance and accounting roles, including Bookkeepers, Senior Bookkeepers, Accountants, and related positions.

- Source, screen, and interview candidates across the U.S. and the Philippines.

- Partner with hiring managers to clarify role needs and support workforce planning as teams grow.

- Build and continuously improve recruitment processes suitable for a startup environment.

- Coordinate interviews, reference checks, and offer processes.

- Maintain the applicant tracking system and ensure a smooth, professional candidate experience.



**HR Setup, Processes & Administration

- Help build and formalize HR processes, policies, and documentation from the ground up.

- Manage onboarding and offboarding processes, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and a strong first and last experience for tea
mbers.

- Maintain and update HR records, templates, and internal guidelines as the company scales.

- Handle HR-related requests, documentation, and internal communications.



**Tea
mber Relations & Benefits Management

- Serve as the primary point of contact for tea
mber questions, concerns, and support.

- Administer benefits programs, including paid time off, leave requests, and other people-related benefits.

- Support a positive, respectful, and compliant work environment for both remote and on-site tea
mbers.



**Performance, Growth & Talent Development

- Coordinate performance review cycles and support the rollout of feedback and development frameworks.

- Partner with leaders to provide guidance on coaching, performance conversations, and growth planning.

- Support career development and internal mobility initiatives as teams expand.



**Process Improvement & HR Partnership

- Identify opportunities to streamline, automate, and improve HR workflows.

- Support HR system implementation and enhancements.

- Act as a strategic HR partner to leadership, offering people insights and recommendations as the organization grows.



Your Skills & Qualifications

- 3+ years of experience as an HR Generalist or in a similar HR role.

- Proven experience managing both contractor and employee relationships, particularly within the U.S.

- Hands-on experience in full-cycle recruitment across the U.S. and the Philippines.

- Proven success in recruiting for finance and accounting roles.

- Solid understanding of U.S. labor laws and HR compliance, including contractor-based models.

- Comfortable working in a startup or fast-growth environment where processes are being built and refined.

- Strong communication, problem-solving, and stakeholder management skills.

- Ability to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic and evolving environment.



What’s in it for You?

- 100% Remote Work – Support teams from anywhere in the Philippines

- Career Growth – Be part of a growing company with opportunities to help build teams and advance your HR career

- Competitive Pay – $8/hour + benefits as an independent contractor

- Paid Time Off – 7 days of PTO per year + paid holidays (100% extra pay if worked)

- Medical Aid Bonus – Monthly medical stipend

- Supportive Culture – Work closely with leaders in a collaborative, growth-oriented environment



If you’re excited about building, scaling, and shaping HR in a startup setting, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Bankruptcy Filing Paralegal (Full-Time Remote)
description:
We are seeking an experienced U.S. Bankruptcy Filing Paralegal whose primary responsibility is preparing and filing complete, accurate bankruptcy petitions.



This position is for someone who already knows how to draft and finalize cases. You will not be collecting documents from clients. You will receive organized files and will be responsible for preparing, reviewing, and filing petitions with precision.



This is not an entry-level role. U.S. debtor-side bankruptcy experience is required.



Overview



You will prepare Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 cases from organized client documents through final petition filing. Accuracy, speed, and attention to detail are critical.



Key Responsibilities



• Prepare Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 petitions, schedules, and statements

• Draft Chapter 13 plans and required local forms

• Review organized financial documents for internal consistency

• Calculate income, expenses, means test, and disposable income

• Identify discrepancies before filing

• Prepare amendments when necessary

• Finalize cases for attorney review and filing

• Monitor filing deadlines

• Maintain clean, organized electronic case files



This role is focused on production and precision.



Performance Expectations



• Accurate preparation of assigned cases with minimal corrections

• Filing readiness within designated timeline

• Low rejection/error rate

• Strong understanding of trustee requirements

• Consistent volume handling without sacrificing quality



Requirements



• Minimum 3 years of U.S. debtor-side bankruptcy paralegal experience

• Strong experience with Chapters 7 and 13 (Chapter 11 experience is a plus)

• Ability to independently draft full petitions and schedules

• Experience responding to trustee objections and preparing amendments

• Familiarity with Best Case or similar bankruptcy software

• Strong written English

• Detail-oriented and deadline-driven



Compensation



Competitive monthly compensation based on experience and production capacity.



To Apply



Please include:



1. Your years of U.S. bankruptcy experience

2. The chapters you have drafted independently

3. Estimated number of cases you can prepare per month

4. Software you have used (Best Case, etc.)

5. Your expected monthly compensation

6. Your availability



Subject line: FILING PARALEGAL



We are looking for professionals who take pride in filing clean, complete petitions that require minimal revisions.

title: Instagram DM Setter — Online Tennis Fitness Coaching Brand (Part-Time, 2 Hours/Day)
description:
To apply you must begin your message with the word: SERVE

Applications without this word will not be considered.



About the Role

I run CourtFit, an online tennis fitness coaching brand with 13k Instagram followers targeting recreational tennis players aged 30-55. I need a dedicated DM setter to message new followers, reactivate cold conversations, and respond to inbound enquiries using a proven script. Your job is to move warm leads toward booking a discovery call with me.

This is not a customer service role. You need to hold natural qualifying conversations that build trust and make booking feel like the obvious next step.



What You'll Do

Message new followers who come in from content and ads within a set daily window. Reactivate previous conversations that have gone cold. Respond to all inbound DMs and story replies. Follow a provided conversation framework naturally rather than copy pasting. Book interested prospects into my Acuity calendar. Flag hot leads or sensitive conversations for me personally. Update a simple daily tracking sheet.



How the Hours Work

Two separate one hour shifts daily rather than one block. First hour in the morning to send new follower messages and catch overnight replies. Second hour in the afternoon to follow up on replies and push warm leads toward booking. This works with how people actually use Instagram rather than wasting time waiting for responses.



What I Need From You

Minimum 2 years Instagram DM setting experience. Fitness or coaching niche experience strongly preferred. Natural conversational written English — you must write in someone elses voice. Experience qualifying leads and handling objections through DM. Available for two consistent one hour shifts at UK times daily.



Hours and Pay

Two one hour shifts daily five days per week at $6-9/hr depending on experience. Long term role for the right person.

To Apply



Start your message with SERVE then answer the following



How many years have you worked as an Instagram DM setter and what niches have you worked in

Have you worked for a fitness or coaching brand before and if yes describe exactly what you did

Someone messages you out of the blue saying saw your post about tennis fitness Ive been struggling with my movement on court for years not sure if I need a coach or a trainer — write your response exactly as you would send it



Applications without the keyword and all three answers will not be considered
