title: Assistant E-commerce Director
description:
We operate three profitable E-commerce stores and are currently launching our fourth. As we continue to scale, we’re looking for a highly experienced E-commerce Assistant Store Manager to handle day-to-day operations, support customers across multiple channels, and help keep our systems running smoothly.



This is a senior-level role with long-term potential for the right candidate. Please read the full job description and answer all questions for your application to be considered.



IMPORTANT: Please read the entire job description and follow the application instructions carefully to be considered.



Responsibilities

• Manage customer inquiries via phone,
, and chat with professionalism and accuracy.

• Handle RFQs, returns/exchanges, complaints, disputes, and negative reviews.

• Collect and maintain positive customer reviews and overall satisfaction.

• Support sales-related tasks and assist in improving store performance.

• Collaborate with the CS, Orders, and Product teams to share insights and ensure a consistent customer experience.

• Identify process gaps, recommend improvements, and contribute to SOP updates.

• Perform daily administrative tasks, including EOS reports and operational updates.

• Proactively manage assigned tasks without waiting for instructions.



What You Need to Succeed -



Required Experience:

• 2+ years in E-commerce operations (customer service + order management + product management).

• Proven Shopify experience (listings, inventory, bulk updates, app usage).

• Strong phone communication skills and ability to handle challenging conversations calmly.

• Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

• Strong attention to detail (include the word mushroom in your application).



Technical Requirements:

• Proficiency in Shopify, ClickUp, Airtable, CRM tools, and Google Workspace.

• Reliable computer + 20 Mbps+ internet.

• Backup solutions for both power and internet outages.



Work Style:

• Self-directed - you take initiative and solve problems independently.

• Detail-oriented with a focus on accuracy.

• Customer-focused - complaints are opportunities to improve systems.

• Process-minded - you document and refine workflows naturally.



Work Schedule:

• Full-time, 40 hours/week

• US business hours: 9 PM–6 AM or 10 PM–7 AM PHT

• 1-hour break

• Five days per week



You're NOT a Fit If:

• You need micromanagement or detailed daily task lists

• You can't work 9 PM–7 AM PHT consistently

• You experience frequent power/internet outages without backup solutions

• You view this as temporary income between other opportunities



Before You Apply - Please Confirm:

• Weekly payments for the first 4 weeks (starting from week 3), then bi-monthly every other Tuesday.

• Full-time role, US hours.

• Must be open to a two-month trial period.

• Daily End of Shift (EOS) report answering:

a) What did you do today? (detailed description)

b) What issues did you run into?

c) Anything you need help with?

If you agree to all terms, write “I AGREE TO ALL TERMS” and answer the questions below.



Application Instructions (Follow Exactly) -



Subject Line:

[Your First Name] – I Am Your New E-commerce Assistant Store Manager

Example: Kevin – E-commerce Assistant Store Manager



Submit:

• Cover letter (focused on relevant e-commerce experience)

• Resume



Answer These Questions (Complete Sentences Required):

1. Describe your most relevant e-commerce operations experience (platforms used, team size, order volume, responsibilities).

2. What specific Shopify tasks have you performed?

3. What are your current work commitments and availability?

4. Provide your internet speed (include screenshot), computer specifications, and backup solutions for power & internet issues.

5. Provide one example of a difficult customer situation you handled and how you resolved it.

6. Why are you interested in this specific role instead of other VA positions?



Important: AI-generated or template responses will be immediately disqualified. We're looking for thoughtful, specific answers that demonstrate you read and understood this posting.

title: PPC Manager (Google Ads & Microsoft Ads)

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

Google Ads certification and agency experience are a plus.

title: PPC Campaign Manager
description:
Role Overview

We are looking for an experienced PPC Campaign Manager to join our digital marketing operations team. Our company specializes in performance marketing for top Shopify App Store applications, and we need a skilled professional to manage campaigns for our established client portfolio.



Primary Responsibilities:

-Manage systematic campaign procedures across 20+ active client accounts.

-Monitor key campaign performance metrics and implement optimization strategies.

-Adjust campaigns by refining keywords, optimizing bids, and managing budgets.

-Prepare standardized weekly performance summaries and detailed monthly reports.

-Communicate regularly with clients regarding campaign performance and strategy.

-Operate proprietary campaign automation tools according to established protocols.

-Identify opportunities for process improvement and suggest actionable strategies.

-Maintain accurate documentation and track campaign performance effectively





Mandatory Qualifications:

-Proven experience in PPC campaign management (Google Ads,
 Ads, or similar platforms).

-Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret campaign performance metrics.

-Proficiency in key campaign KPIs, including ROAS, CPA, CTR, and conversion metrics.

-Excellent communication skills in English, both written and verbal.

-Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.

-Demonstrated ability to manage multiple client accounts simultaneously.

-Availability: Sunday-Thursday, 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM Israel time (5 hours daily).



Preferred Qualifications:

-Experience with Shopify or e-commerce advertising.

-Familiarity with marketing automation tools.

-Background in an agency environment or managing multiple client accounts.

-Knowledge of SaaS or subscription-based business models.

-Experience with client reporting tools and methodologies.



Compensation Structure:

-Growth potential: Full-time opportunity for high-performing candidates.

-Reporting: Direct line to the company founder.



Operational Context:

The advertising platform used follows familiar principles (keyword-based campaigns, bidding systems, performance tracking)—similar to Google Ads. Comprehensive onboarding and training will be provided on platform-specific features and proprietary automation systems.



Employment Structure:

Initially, this is a part-time role to ensure a strong fit. Successful performance and mutual satisfaction will lead to the opportunity to transition to a full-time position.



Candidates with strong PPC skills, a systematic approach to campaign management, and a focus on measurable results are encouraged to apply for immediate consideration.

title: Instagram Video Editor
description:

title: AP Manager (Working according to U.S. Eastern Time)
description:
The company is a well-established healthcare organization based in the U.S., specializing in medical devices and patient care. It has a strong global presence and is known for innovation and quality.



We are seeking a skilled and experienced professional to oversee the daily operations of the Accounts Payable (AP) function as an outsourced AP Manager. This role will be responsible for managing AP processes, ensuring timely and accurate vendor payments, maintaining strong internal controls, and leading communication between internal AP teams, external vendors, and the Korean HQ.



Key Responsibilities:

• Oversee end-to-end AP operations, including invoice processing, three-way matching, payment processing, expense management, and vendor master maintenance.

• Ensure timely and accurate processing of vendor invoices and employee expense reimbursements.

• Monitor AP aging reports and manage payment schedules to optimize cash flow while meeting contractual obligations.

• Review and approve invoice coding, ensuring proper GL allocation and compliance with company policies.

• Reconcile AP sub-ledgers to the general ledger and resolve discrepancies promptly.

• Coordinate with Procurement, Operations, and internal accounting teams to resolve invoice discrepancies and vendor issues.

• Maintain and improve AP policies, procedures, and internal controls to ensure compliance and audit readiness.

• Prepare AP-related reports and metrics for management review (e.g., AP aging, payment forecasts, cash disbursement reports).

• Lead monthly AP close activities and support internal and external audit requests as needed.

• Act as point of contact for AP matters, both internally and externally.

• Communicate regularly with Korean HQ American Team regarding AP status, cash disbursements, outstanding liabilities, and reporting requirements.

• Provide periodic AP reports and updates in the format and timeline required by Korean HQ.

• Support Korean HQ in any special AP-related analysis or inquiries.

• Train and oversee any supporting AP staff (if applicable).



Qualifications:

• 5+ years of Accounts Payable or procure-to-pay (P2P) experience, including supervisory or managerial responsibility.

• Strong understanding of U.S. GAAP, accrual accounting, and AP best practices.

• Experience managing full-cycle AP processes, including invoice processing, payment runs, reconciliations, and vendor management.

• Experience with ERP/accounting systems (e.g., SAP, QuickBooks, or similar ERP platforms).

• Strong Excel skills, including data analysis, reconciliations, and reporting.

• Knowledge of internal controls, audit support, and compliance requirements.

• Excellent communication and problem-solving abilities.

• Detail-oriented, highly organized, and able to manage multiple priorities in a deadline-driven environment.

• Experience communicating and coordinating with international teams, particularly Korean HQ preferred.

• Experience in a multi-entity or global organization is a plus.

title: Marketing Researcher
description:
Company: BriarBear

Location: Fully Remote (Philippines)

Schedule: Graveyard Shift (Start time between 5:00 PM - 7:00 PM PH Time)

Employment Type: Full-Time



About this Role

We are hiring a Marketing Researcher to own and drive long-burn, compounding organic

growth for a portfolio of AI-powered digital products.

BriarBear builds consumer products, SaaS tools, and mobile apps with AI at their core—AI

chatbots with proprietary knowledge bases, intelligent workflows, and AI-powered matching

systems. These products live or die by distribution.

While some roles on our team focus on short-form content and social platforms, this role is

responsible for the slower, deeper, compounding channels:

? SEO & organic search

? Blogs (strategy, not writing)

?
 campaigns

? Pinterest

? App Store Optimization (ASO)

? Authority, backlinks, and PR-driven discoverability

This is not a writing role.

This is a strategy, systems, and accountability role for someone who thinks like a growth

engineer—someone who can turn traffic data, rankings, and funnels into clear plans and

measurable outcomes.

You are responsible for d
 what we should rank for, why it matters, how we get there,

and how we measure success.



What this Role is Accountable For

This role is accountable for real business outcomes, not output:

? Traffic growth

? Search rankings

? Conversion rates

? Authority and visibility

? Revenue impact

Views without conversion are failure, not success.



What You’ll Be Responsible For

1. Own Long-Burn Organic Growth Strategy

Develop and continuously refine organic growth strategies across:

? Google Search (SEO)

? Blogs (topic selection, optimization, performance)

?
 campaigns

? Pinterest

? App Store rankings (ASO)

? Emerging discovery surfaces (AI search / LLMs)

You are expected to understand why something ranks, not just that it ranks.

2. Translate Business Goals Into Measurable Growth Targets

You will take high-level goals such as:

? “Increase organic traffic from 20K ? 100K monthly users”

? “Rank for high-intent keywords in X category”

? “Increase app store installs from organic search”

And translate them into:

? Keyword targets and ranking goals

? Traffic and conversion benchmarks

? Funnel assumptions (impressions ? clicks ? users ? customers)

? Clear milestones and timelines

You are accountable for defining the strategy and path forward.

3. SEO, Keywords, and Authority Strategy

You will:

? Analyze current traffic sources and performance

? Identify keyword opportunities worth pursuing (and which are not)

? Build keyword maps and content opportunity lists

? D
 what content should exist to capture search demand

? Optimize existing content to improve rankings and conversion

This includes authority-building strategy:

? Backlink acquisition (organic, partnerships, PR, tools like Outrank, etc.)

? D
 whether to pursue media placements, PR, or direct backlink strategies

? Building topical authority in our category

You will help answer questions like:

? What keywords are we ranking for today?

? What keywords should we rank for next?



? How do we become a trusted authority in this space?

4. Funnel Thinking & Conversion Optimization

Design and test strategies that convert:

? Search impressions ? website visits

? Website visits ? signups

? Signups ? paying customers

D
 and test:

? Content intent (informational vs. transactional)

? Page structure and optimization

? Website vs. app store routing

?
 sequencing and timing

Monitor conversion rates at each stage and adjust strategy accordingly.

5. Measurement, Reporting, and Iteration

Track and report on:

? Traffic by channel

? Keyword rankings

? Conversion rates

? Performance by content category

You will:

? Build dashboards and reports leadership can understand

? Identify what’s working and double down

? Kill underperforming strategies quickly

? Diagnose why growth stalls and propose fixes

You own the outcome.

6. Work With (But Do Not Replace) Creative & Writing Resources

You will not be expected to write blogs or long-form content yourself.

Instead, you will:

? Provide clear briefs to writers or creators

? Define keyword intent, structure, and success criteria

? Review performance and iterate based on data



Who You Are

? Strategic thinker with a strong bias toward execution

? Analytical and systems-oriented

? Comfortable owning numbers and being accountable for results



? Understands SEO, authority, and ranking mechanics deeply or willing to learn

? Can explain why a strategy should work

? Organized, detail-oriented, and process-driven

? Comfortable working directly with leadership



Nice to Have (But Not Required)

? Proven experience in SEO, growth marketing, or performance marketing

? Strong understanding of:

? Search intent

? Keywords and rankings

? Funnels and conversion metrics

? Experience analyzing traffic and performance data

? Familiarity with ASO (App Store Optimization)

? Experience with backlink or PR-driven SEO

? Data analysis or experimentation background

? Interest in AI search and LLM discoverability



Why This Role Matters

This role directly determines:

? Whether long-term organic growth compounds or stalls

? Whether content becomes an asset or a cost

? Whether BriarBear becomes an authority in its category

? Whether distribution works without relying solely on paid ads



Click here to apply:

title: Lead Generation Manager
description:
Job Title  

Lead Generation Manager  

(B2B Services, Full-Time)  




  



---



About the role  



We run a profitable WordPress support and services business with an established client base and a strong delivery team. What we are deliberately building next is a predictable, well-run lead generation function.



This role exists to own that outcome.



We are looking for a senior lead generation professional who has already built and run B2B lead generation for services or agencies, and who is comfortable taking responsibility for both strategy and execution.



This is not a junior SDR role, and not a growth hacker position. It is a role for someone who understands that good lead generation is systematic, evidence-led, and improves through disciplined iteration.



---



What you will own  



- Defining and refining our ideal customer profile for services and support  

- Designing and running outbound and content-led lead generation  

- Building and managing prospect lists  

- Writing, testing, and improving outreach messaging  

- Managing follow-ups and responses  

- Booking qualified conversations with the founder  

- Tracking performance and reporting signal clearly  

- Recommending what to scale, what to stop, and what to change  



You are expected to take ownership of this function and to challenge assumptions when needed.



---



What success looks like  



Success in this role is not about vanity metrics or activity for its own sake.



It looks like:



- Clear, well-reasoned decisions about who we target and why  

- Messaging that improves based on real-world feedback  

- A growing number of relevant, qualified conversations over time  

- Honest reporting on what is working and what is not  

- Confidence that the approach can be scaled once proven  



You will be expected to show progress through clarity, learning, and outcomes, not just volume.



---



Required experience (non-negotiable)  



To be considered, you must have:



- Direct experience running B2B lead generation for services or agencies  

- Experience selling professional services, retainers, or support-style offerings  

- Proven ability to design and run outbound campaigns end-to-end  

- Strong written and verbal communication skills  

- Experience reporting performance to founders or senior leadership  



Experience in ecommerce, consumer products, or purely B2C environments is not sufficient for this role.



---



Working style  



- Full-time, remote  

- High autonomy and clear ownership  

- Direct communication with the founder and Ops Lead  

- Honest reporting expected, even when results are uncomfortable  



---



How to apply  



Use the following link:

title: Relationship & Client Follow-Up Virtual Assistant (Legal Focus)
description:
We are seeking a highly organized, professional, and proactive Virtual Assistant with legal and CRM experience to manage relationship follow-up, referral outreach, and client booking efforts. This role is centered around nurturing relationships, increasing booked meetings, improving response times, and helping convert conversations into retained clients. The ideal candidate is confident in communication, experienced with CRM systems, and understands the professionalism required in a legal environment.





Key Responsibilities

1. Relationship & Referral Follow-Up

* Maintain and update all referral partner and prospect contacts in CRM

* Proactively follow up with attorneys, referral sources, and past connections

* Schedule and confir
etings with potential partners and clients

* Track follow-up cadence to ensure no opportunity falls through the cracks



2. Client Intake & Booking Support

* Respond to inbound inquiries in a timely and professional manner

* Pre-qualify leads based on established criteria

* Book consultations for attorneys

* Ensure intake forms and documentation are completed prior to meetings



3. CRM Management & Pipeline Tracking

* Maintain CRM accuracy and cleanliness

* Track pipeline stages (lead ? consultation ? retained client)

* Set reminders and follow-up tasks

* Provide weekly reporting on booked meetings and conversion activity



4. Outreach & Communication

* Conduct
 and phone outreach to warm prospects and referral partners

* Send follow-up messages after networking events or introductions

* Draft professional communication templates

* Maintain confidentiality and adhere to legal communication standards



Qualifications:

* Prior experience working with a law firm or legal environment (strongly preferred)

* Experience with CRM systems (HubSpot, Clio, Lawmatics, Go High Level, etc.)

* Strong written and verbal English communication skills

* Comfortable making follow-up phone calls

* Organized, proactive, and detail-oriented

* Understanding of legal client confidentiality and professionalism



Key Traits for Success:

* Professional and polished communicator

* Persistent but respectful in follow-ups

* Comfortable tracking metrics and accountability

* Relationship-oriented, not transactional

* High integrity and confidentiality mindset



To fast track your application, please submit the following information:

Resume: A link to your Google Drive. Please ensure the sharing setting is set to "Anyone with the link."

title: Home Care / Home Health VA
description:
About the Role

We are seeking a reliable and detail-oriented Home Care Back Office Support professional to assist with daily administrative and operational functions within a Home Care setting. This role plays a key part in supporting care delivery by coordinating caregiver schedules, assisting with recruitment efforts, and helping maintain continuity of client services.



This position is strictly for candidates with prior Home Care or Home Health agency operations experience. Applicants without direct industry experience will not be considered. Experience in caregiver scheduling and recruitment within a Home Care or Home Health agency is required and considered a significant advantage.



We are specifically looking for professionals who understand the fast-paced nature of Home Care operations, compliance requirements, and caregiver coordination workflows.



Key Responsibilities



Operations Support

Assist with the coordination of client service requests

Maintain accurate caregiver and client records

Communicate with field staff regarding assignments and schedule updates

Monitor active schedules and report coverage gaps

Support onboarding documentation and compliance tracking



Scheduling

Coordinate caregiver schedules based on client needs

Arrange shift coverage for call-offs or new cases

Confirm assignments and communicate scheduling updates

Track attendance and report missed visits or tardiness

Maintain scheduling system accuracy



Recruiting Support

Assist in sourcing caregiver applicants

Coordinate applicant interviews

Support the onboarding documentation process

Maintain applicant tracking records

Assist with job posting updates as needed



Qualifications

High School Diploma or equivalent required

Required: Prior experience working in a Home Care or Home Health agency (operations, scheduling, or staffing capacity)

Proven experience in caregiver scheduling required

Experience in recruiting or onboarding support preferred

Prior administrative or staffing experience preferred

Strong organizational and communication skills

Ability to handle confidential information appropriately

Basic proficiency in office software systems



Compliance & Confidentiality

This position may involve access to confidential client information and requires adherence to the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy and Security Rules. All employees must comply with agency confidentiality policies and complete required training upon hire.



Work Environment

Remote work

Frequent communication via phone and computer

Ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced Home Care operations environment



How to Apply

INCLUDE YOUR RESUME on your application.

Applications without relevant Home Care or Home Health experience will not be considered.

title: DM Outreach Setter
description:
You will be responsible for Instagram DM outreach for a filmmaking company specialized in high quality video editing for Muslim entrepreneurs, speakers, podcasts, events and organizations.

Your responsibilities:

• Research and identify qualified Muslim businesses

• Send approximately 20 personalized DMs per day (4 days per week)

• Maintain respectful and professional communication

• Offer 1–3 complimentary test edits

• Document all outreach activity in a structured Excel sheet



Preferred but not mandatory:

We primarily work with Muslim entrepreneurs and organizations. Familiarity with Islamic values, terminology, and etiquette is highly preferred, as it helps ensure respectful and culturally aligned communication.



This is not a spam based role. We value quality, etiquette and professionalism.

Initial workload is approximately 8 hours per week. If collaboration works well, we would be happy to expand responsibilities over time.
